The section-level identification
Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange} below is the single
highest-leverage obligation of the affine close, and we prove it \emph{directly on
global sections}, with no abstract adjoint-mate tower. In the affine--affine model the
base-change map \(\theta\) is, after its domain and codomain are read through the two
proved tilde dictionaries, a concrete \(R'\)-linear map
\(R' \otimes_R M \to (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\). The active route — the
\emph{explicit-inverse + element-extensionality} argument of
\cref{subsec:section_explicit_inverse} — supplies a two-sided inverse for this map directly from
the regrouping isomorphism \((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M \cong R' \otimes_R M\)
(Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}) and checks the two round-trips on pure tensors,
\(r' \otimes m\) and \(r' \otimes (a \otimes m)\), by an element chase. Because every object of the
affine--affine model is the \(\operatorname{Spec}\) of a ring and every module a tilde-module, the
global-sections functor is conservative, so bijectivity on these pure tensors upgrades to an
isomorphism of \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}(R')\)-objects; the conjugate (mate) normal form is never
produced. The computation factors through the domain read, the codomain read, the explicit regroup
inverse, and the one Mathlib-absent crux identifying the pullback cone legs with the
\(\operatorname{Spec}\)-maps of the tensor inclusions
(Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor}). Two Mathlib results are recorded
first as dependency anchors.

The section-level identification
Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange} below is the single
highest-leverage obligation of the affine close. In the affine--affine model the
base-change map \(\theta\) is, after its domain and codomain are read through the two
proved tilde dictionaries, a concrete \(R'\)-linear map
\(R' \otimes_R M \to (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\), and the content of the lemma is
that this map is the inverse of the cancellation isomorphism
\(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\). Because the base-change map is \emph{defined} as the
transpose of the inner pushforward comparison \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\) under the
\((g^* \dashv g_*)\)-adjunction (\(g = \operatorname{Spec}\psi\)), its section-level value
is read off by the \emph{conjugate-counit calculus}: unfolding the transpose through the
counit form of the hom-set adjunction
(\(\operatorname{Adjunction.homEquiv\_counit}\)) factors \(\theta\) as
\(g^*(\theta_{\mathrm{in}})\) followed by the geometric counit \(\varepsilon_g\), and
conjugating this composite by the two tilde dictionaries
\(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read}) and
\(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}) replaces the
geometric counit by the \emph{algebraic} extension-of-scalars counit along \(\psi\). This
is the route the live formalization takes: the cancellation identity
Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange} routes through the generator
trace Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_generator_trace}, which is a one-line corollary of the
section identity Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_section_identity}, whose single residual
obligation is the counit-conjugate coherence Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_gstar_transpose}.
The per-generator/element evaluation is \emph{not} available as a shortcut: evaluating the
opaque geometric composite on \(1 \otimes m\) produces no element-level normal form that
\(\operatorname{rfl}\) or \(\operatorname{simp}\) can reduce, so the abstract conjugate
calculus is the only tractable discharge. Two Mathlib results are recorded
first as dependency anchors.

\begin{lemma}[Transposed counit-across-conjugate coherence]
  \label{lem:conjugateEquiv_counit_symm_mathlib}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv_counit_symm}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  Let \(\mathrm{adj}_1 : L_1 \dashv R_1\) and \(\mathrm{adj}_2 : L_2 \dashv R_2\) be adjunctions with
  counits \(\varepsilon_1,\varepsilon_2\), and let \(\alpha : L_2 \Rightarrow L_1\) be a natural
  transformation of the left adjoints. Then for every object \(d\),
  \[
    L_2\bigl(\alpha_d\bigr) \,\circ\, (\varepsilon_2)_d
      \;=\; \bigl(\bigl(\operatorname{conj}(\mathrm{adj}_1,\mathrm{adj}_2)^{-1}\alpha\bigr)\bigr)_d
        \,\circ\, (\varepsilon_1)_d ,
  \]
  the counit dual of the transposed unit-across-conjugate coherence
  Lemma~\ref{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_symm_mathlib}. This is the master tool of the conjugate-counit
  discharge: instantiated at the composite adjunctions
  \(\mathrm{adj}_L = (\widetilde{(-)} \dashv \Gamma)_R \cdot ((\operatorname{Spec}\psi)^* \dashv
  (\operatorname{Spec}\psi)_*)\) and
  \(\mathrm{adj}_R = (\operatorname{ext}_\psi \dashv \operatorname{restr}_\psi) \cdot
  (\widetilde{(-)} \dashv \Gamma)_{R'}\) with \(\alpha = \beta^{\mathrm{hom}}\) the pushforward
  \(\Gamma\)-comparison (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardNatIso}), it transports the geometric counit
  \(\varepsilon_g\) (conjugated by the pullback dictionary
  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso} and the tilde counit) to the algebraic

  identity \(\mathrm{huce}\) consumed by Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_gstar_transpose}.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read,
        lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv,
        lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso, lem:base_change_mate_gstar_counit_transport,
        lem:base_change_mate_inner_value_eq,
        lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs,
        lem:base_change_mate_gstar_generator_close}
  This is the closing step of the affine section-level computation; the section
  identity Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_section_identity} follows from it directly. The
  argument is the counit dual of Seam~1, in four moves.

  \emph{Counit factorization.} The base-change map
  (Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map}) is the \((g^* \dashv g_*)\)-transpose of
  \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\) (\(g = \operatorname{Spec}\psi\)); by the counit form of the hom-set
  adjunction it factors as \(g^*(\theta_{\mathrm{in}})\) followed by the counit \(\varepsilon_g\),
  with no opaque transpose remaining,
  \(\Gamma(\theta) = \Gamma\bigl(g^*(\theta_{\mathrm{in}})\bigr) \circ \Gamma(\varepsilon_g)\).
  Splitting by functoriality of \(\Gamma\) and moving the domain read \(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}\),
  the regrouping isomorphism, and the codomain read \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\) to the right leaves
  on the left exactly the two geometric factors
  \(\Gamma_{R'}\bigl(g^*(\theta_{\mathrm{in}})\bigr) \circ \Gamma_{R'}(\varepsilon_g)\). Because
  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}\), \(\operatorname{regroup}\) and \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\) are themselves
  conjugate-side comparisons, this splitting is performed on the conjugate side — applying injectivity
  of the conjugation bijection and the reassoc conjugate coherences — rather than by a positional
  head-symbol cancellation under the \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module instance diamond.

  \emph{Counit transport (Seam~C).} By
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_gstar_counit_transport}, conjugating the geometric counit
  \(\Gamma_{R'}(\varepsilon_g)\) by the tilde/pullback dictionaries that \(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read}) and \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}) bake in — the pullback dictionary
  \(\Pi_{\psi}\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso} together with the tilde--\(\Gamma\)
  counit — replaces it by the algebraic counit \(\varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}\) of the
  extension/restriction-of-scalars adjunction along \(\psi\). The conjugated transpose becomes
  \(\operatorname{ext}_\psi(\,\cdot\,) \circ \varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}\) applied to the
  \(\operatorname{Spec} R\)-section reading of \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\).

  \emph{Inner value (Seam~A).} The \(\operatorname{Spec} R\)-section reading of
  \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\) is the canonical \(R\)-linear map
  \(\rho : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\) (Definition~\ref{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}),
  recorded as Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_inner_value_eq}. This inner value is not obtained by a
  direct section computation: it routes through the reindex
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs}, taken at the explicit composite leg and proved
  on the conjugate side (Open Q2). With the inner value available the residual \(g^*\)-leg, conjugated
  by \(\Pi_{\psi}\), is \(\operatorname{ext}_\psi(\rho)\).

  \emph{Generator close (Seam~B).} By
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_gstar_generator_close} the composite
  \(\operatorname{ext}_\psi(\rho) \circ \varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}\) equals the inverse regrouping
  isomorphism \(\operatorname{regroup}^{-1}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}) — both
  \(R'\)-linear and agreeing on \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\). This is the
  claimed equation.
\end{proof}

\begin{proof}

  This is the closing step of the affine section-level computation; the section
  identity Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_section_identity} follows from it directly.

  \emph{Counit factorization and the master transport identity.} The base-change map
  (Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map}) is the \((g^* \dashv g_*)\)-transpose of
  \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\) (\(g = \operatorname{Spec}\psi\)); by the counit form of the hom-set
  adjunction it factors as \(g^*(\theta_{\mathrm{in}})\) followed by the geometric counit
  \(\varepsilon_g\), with no opaque transpose remaining,
  \(\Gamma(\theta) = \Gamma_{R'}\bigl(g^*(\theta_{\mathrm{in}})\bigr) \circ \Gamma_{R'}(\varepsilon_g)\).
  Instantiating the transposed counit-across-conjugate coherence
  Lemma~\ref{lem:conjugateEquiv_counit_symm_mathlib} at the two composite adjunctions
  \(\mathrm{adj}_L = (\widetilde{(-)}_R \dashv \Gamma_R) . (g^* \dashv g_*)\) and
  \(\mathrm{adj}_R = (\operatorname{ext}_\psi \dashv \operatorname{restr}_\psi) .
  (\widetilde{(-)}_{R'} \dashv \Gamma_{R'})\) with \(\alpha = \beta^{\mathrm{hom}}\) the pushforward
  \(\Gamma\)-comparison, and fusing the two composite-counit splits of \(\mathrm{adj}_L\) and
  \(\mathrm{adj}_R\), yields the \emph{master counit-transport identity}
  \[
    \widetilde{(-)}_{R'}\!\bigl(\varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}\bigr) \circ \varepsilon^{\Gamma}
    \;=\;
    \Pi_{\psi}^{-1} \circ g^*\!\bigl(\varepsilon^{\Gamma}\bigr) \circ \varepsilon_g ,
  \]
  where \(\varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}\) is the algebraic extension/restriction-of-scalars counit
  along \(\psi\), \(\varepsilon^{\Gamma}\) the tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit, and \(\Pi_{\psi}\) the
  pullback dictionary (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}); the dictionary enters because the
  conjugate of \(\beta^{\mathrm{hom}}\) is \(\Pi_{\psi}^{-1}\). The trailing dictionary and
  tilde-counit factors are isomorphisms, so solving this identity for \(\varepsilon_g\) and applying
  \(\Gamma_{R'}\) rewrites \(\Gamma_{R'}(\varepsilon_g)\) in the goal into the algebraic counit
  conjugated by exactly the dictionaries that \(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read}) and \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}) bake in. The remaining \(\sim\)150 lines of
  telescoping are three steps.

  \emph{(a) Inner reindex} (\(\Gamma_R(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}) = \rho\)). The \(\operatorname{Spec} R\)
  section reading of the inner comparison \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\) is the canonical \(R\)-linear map
  \(\rho : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\) (the affine inner value). This is reproven
  \emph{inline}, not cited from the leg-reindex wrapper: expand the buried \((g')\)-unit by the
  leg-unit expansion Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand} (whose engine is
  the proved unit-distribution Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackPushforward_unit_comp}), distribute the
  resulting four factors through \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\) and \(\Gamma\) by
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_gammaDistribute}, collapse the
  pushforward-composition coherences to the identity \(\Gamma\)-image
  (Lemmas~\ref{lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_hom_eq_id},
  \ref{lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_inv_eq_id}), and evaluate the lone surviving affine unit by
  Seam~1 (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}); the result is \(\rho\). With the inner value
  available the residual \(g^*\)-leg, conjugated by \(\Pi_{\psi}\), is
  \(\operatorname{ext}_\psi(\rho)\).

  \emph{(b) One-generator close.} The composite
  \(\operatorname{ext}_\psi(\rho) \circ \varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}\) is an \(R'\)-linear map
  \(R' \otimes_R M \to (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\); by \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}\)
  extensionality it suffices to evaluate it on the generator \(r' \otimes m\), where it returns
  \(r' \cdot ((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m) = (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\). This is exactly the value of the
  inverse regrouping isomorphism \(\operatorname{regroup}^{-1}\) on \(r' \otimes m\), so a
  one-generator \(\operatorname{ext}\) against Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv} gives
  \(\operatorname{ext}_\psi(\rho) \circ \varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}} = \operatorname{regroup}^{-1}\).

  \emph{(c) Dictionary cancellation.} It remains to match the \(\Pi_{\psi}\) (pullback dictionary,
  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}) and tilde-counit factors appearing in the master
  transport identity against the \(\operatorname{pushforward}\)- and \(\operatorname{pullback}\)-tilde
  dictionaries baked into the domain read \(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}\) and codomain read
  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\). These factors are mutually inverse isomorphisms, and cancelling them
  reduces the conjugated geometric transpose to the algebraic composite of step~(b), namely
  \(\operatorname{regroup}^{-1}\). This is the claimed equation
  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1} \ggg \Gamma(\theta) \ggg \Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}
  = \operatorname{regroup}^{-1}\).
\end{proof}

\subsection{The section-level isomorphism via an explicit inverse}
\label{subsec:section_explicit_inverse}

This is the active route for the section-level identity (obligation~2). It replaces the abandoned
conjugate-side / adjoint-mate discharge (now flagged superseded above). The idea is elementary: in the
fully-affine model the global-sections functor on quasi-coherent
\((\operatorname{Spec} R')\)-modules is conservative — every object is the \(\operatorname{Spec}\) of a
ring and every module a tilde-module — so a \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}(R')\)-morphism is an
isomorphism precisely when it is bijective on underlying modules. We exhibit a two-sided inverse for
the section-level base-change map and verify the two round-trips on pure tensors by an element chase,
so the conjugate (mate) normal form is never produced and the \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\) instance diamond
is never crossed.

Throughout, \(\theta\) denotes the conjugated section-level base-change map
\[
  \theta \;=\; \Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1} \;\ggg\; \Gamma(\alpha) \;\ggg\; \Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}
  \;:\; R' \otimes_R M \longrightarrow (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M ,
\]
read through the domain identification \(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}\)
(Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read}) and the codomain identification
\(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}); on the generator it sends
\(r' \otimes m \mapsto (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m\), computed by unwinding the base-change map on the
pure tensor through the two proved affine dictionaries and the cone-leg identification
(Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor}).

\begin{definition}
[Explicit inverse of the section-level base-change map]
  \label{def:base_change_mate_section_inverse}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_section_inverse}
  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv, lem:base_change_mate_domain_read,
        lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}
  The candidate inverse of \(\theta\) is the regrouping isomorphism
  \[
    \iota \;=\; \operatorname{regroup} \;:\;
    (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M
  \]
  of Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}, which sends
  \((r' \otimes a) \otimes m \mapsto r' \otimes (a \cdot m)\) and, in the inverse direction,
  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m\). Read at the level of the raw section modules it is
  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}^{-1} \ggg \operatorname{regroup} \ggg \Theta_{\mathrm{src}}\), built from
  \(\operatorname{regroup}\) and the two tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit/dictionary identifications
  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read}) and
  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}). It carries no flatness
  hypothesis.
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}
[Round-trip 1: the base-change map followed by the inverse is the identity]
  \label{lem:base_change_mate_section_map_inverse_id}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_section_map_inverse_id}
  \uses{def:base_change_mate_section_inverse, lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv,
        lem:base_change_mate_domain_read, lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read,
        lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor}
  The composite \(\theta \ggg \iota\) is the identity of \(R' \otimes_R M\):
  \[
    \theta \;\ggg\; \iota \;=\; \operatorname{id}_{R' \otimes_R M} .
  \]
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:base_change_mate_section_inverse, lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv,
        lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor}
  By \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}\)-extensionality it suffices to check equality on the pure tensors
  \(r' \otimes m\) generating \(R' \otimes_R M\). On such a generator the base-change map sends
  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m\) (the generator value of \(\theta\) recorded above,
  obtained by the element chase through the two affine dictionaries and the cone-leg identification
  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor}), and the regrouping inverse
  \(\iota = \operatorname{regroup}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}) then sends
  \((r' \otimes 1) \otimes m \mapsto r' \otimes (1 \cdot m) = r' \otimes m\). Hence the composite fixes
  every generator, so it is the identity.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[Round-trip 2: the inverse followed by the base-change map is the identity]
  \label{lem:base_change_mate_section_inverse_map_id}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_section_inverse_map_id}
  \uses{def:base_change_mate_section_inverse, lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv,
        lem:base_change_mate_domain_read, lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read,
        lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor}
  The composite \(\iota \ggg \theta\) is the identity of \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\):
  \[
    \iota \;\ggg\; \theta \;=\; \operatorname{id}_{(R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M} .
  \]
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:base_change_mate_section_inverse, lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv,
        lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor}
  Again by \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}\)-extensionality it suffices to check equality on the pure
  tensors \((r' \otimes a) \otimes m\) generating \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\). The regrouping
  isomorphism \(\iota = \operatorname{regroup}\) sends \((r' \otimes a) \otimes m \mapsto
  r' \otimes (a \cdot m)\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}), and the base-change map
  then sends \(r' \otimes (a \cdot m) \mapsto (r' \otimes 1) \otimes (a \cdot m)\), which equals
  \((r' \otimes a) \otimes m\) by the \(A\)-balancing of the relative tensor product over \(A\) — the
  scalar \(a\) moves across the middle tensor. Hence the composite fixes every generator, so it is the
  identity.
\end{proof}
